heading: Quarters allowances
label: 5923 Quarters allowances
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 3036, , .Sept. 6, 1960Pub. L. 86–707, § 21174 Stat. 793Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1991— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), substituted “subsistence” for “lodging” and inserted “(including meals and laundry expenses)” after “quarters” in par. (1), substituted “90 days” for “3 months” in par. (1)(A), substituted “30 days” for “1 month” in par. (1)(B), and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 102–1381982—Par. (2). substituted “section 3324(a) and (b)” for “section 529”.Pub. L. 97–258
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 3036, , .Sept. 6, 1960Pub. L. 86–707, § 21174 Stat. 793Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1991— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), substituted “subsistence” for “lodging” and inserted “(including meals and laundry expenses)” after “quarters” in par. (1), substituted “90 days” for “3 months” in par. (1)(A), substituted “30 days” for “1 month” in par. (1)(B), and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 102–1381982—Par. (2). substituted “section 3324(a) and (b)” for “section 529”.Pub. L. 97–258

children.label: A
children.text: for a period not in excess of 90 days after first arrival at a new post of assignment in a foreign area or a period ending with the occupation of residence quarters, whichever is shorter; and

children.label: B
children.text: for a period of not more than 30 days immediately before final departure from the post after the necessary evacuation of residence quarters.
A living quarters allowance for rent, heat, light, fuel, gas, electricity, and water, without regard to section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31.

children.label: A
children.text: the expenses are administratively approved in advance; and

children.label: B
children.text: the duration and terms of the lease justify payment of the expenses by the Government.

children.label: b
children.text: The 90-day period under subsection (a)(1)(A) and the 30-day period under subsection (a)(1)(B) may each be extended for not more than 60 additional days if the head of the agency concerned or his designee determines that there are compelling reasons beyond the control of the employee for the continued occupancy of temporary quarters.
